summary:
The Barthel Index lacks critical dimensions of post-stroke quality of life, such as cognitive function, emotional well-being, and social participation—domains essential for comprehensive assessment. Thus, relying solely on this tool fails to capture important aspects influencing long-term patient outcomes.
